message:
feat(trace): implement logical clocks, DPOR expansion, and diagnostics tests

Distributed tracing:
- trace/distributed/vclock.rs: Full implementation of LogicalClock trait
  with three concrete clock types: LamportClock (total order, atomic),
  VectorClock (partial order, per-node counters with merge), and
  HybridLogicalClock (HLC combining wall time + logical counter for
  bounded clock skew). Includes comprehensive tests for causality,
  merge semantics, and HLC wall-clock advancement.
- trace/distributed/mod.rs: Re-export clock types and trait.
- trace/mod.rs: Wire up distributed module exports.

DPOR (Dynamic Partial Order Reduction):
- trace/dpor.rs: Expand DPOR engine with persistent set computation,
  sleep set optimization, race detection across memory operations,
  and state space reduction statistics. Add backtrack point selection
  with source-set refinement. Tests for race detection accuracy and
  state space bounds.

Observability:
- observability/diagnostics.rs: Replace Duration import with inline
  std::time::Duration to avoid naming conflicts. Add ~400 lines of
  unit tests covering: diagnostic snapshot generation, task leak
  detection, obligation leak detection, deadlock cycle detection,
  region hierarchy analysis, and health scoring.

Lab:
- lab/network/harness.rs: Refactor test harness to use structured
  assertions (assert_with_log!), improve partition simulation with
  bidirectional link tracking, add network statistics.
- lab/runtime.rs: Add lab_runtime_info() diagnostic method.
- lab/virtual_time_wheel.rs: Add tick_count() and pending_timers()
  accessors, refactor advance logic for determinism.

Plan:
- plan/rewrite.rs: Add RewriteRule engine for AST-level plan
  transformations with pattern matching, variable binding, and
  conditional application. Tests for identity rewrites, variable
  capture, and multi-rule composition.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
